title: Equipment Catalog
description: Equipment pieces and the stat modifiers they grant, expressed as DATA per ADR-0005 (DDTank equip effects — AddBombEquipEffect/AddGuardEquipEffect — were flat stat adds applied at Player.Reset; here they are data, not code). Loaded by Sim.Content.EquipmentCatalog.FromJson; hosts read the file, /sim parses the text. Equipment grants stats through the shared modifier stack (Sim.Stats.StatAssembler), resolved into effective CombatantStats at match SETUP by Sim.Stats.LoadoutResolver — never inside the turn loop. Each modifier's provenance (source) is stamped by the parser as Equipment + the piece id, so designers author only stat/op/value. SCOPE (system #4): three slots (Weapon/Armor/Accessory) and a first-pass piece per slot. Modifier ops are flat/additive%/multiplicative% combined in that fixed order. Values are FIRST-PASS TUNING, calibrated to combat.json divisors so effects read as small percentages (BaseGuard 600 -> ~5% guard DR at guardDivisor 12000; Defense 4000 -> 5% defence DR at defenceDivisor 80000; Attack 2500 -> +0.20 attackFactor at attackScale 0.00008) per the anti-inflation pillar (ADR-0006); iterate later. DEFERRED: base stat profile from class/level (progression #6), equipment as ownable inventory (economy #7), rune trees, costumes (cosmetics never grant power), set bonuses, gear score, element-granting weapons.

id.description: Stable lookup key. Must be unique within the catalog.

displayName.description: Display-only label. Has no stat effect.

slot.description: Body slot. First-pass set only; more slots arrive as the system grows.

modifiers.description: Stat modifiers granted by this piece, applied in authored order.

stat.description: The CombatantStats channel this modifier targets. (Element is assigned, not stacked, so it is absent.)

op.description: How it combines. Fixed order: Flat -> AdditivePercent -> MultiplicativePercent. AdditivePercent/MultiplicativePercent values are fractions (0.10 = 10%).

value.description: Magnitude. Flat is added directly; percent ops are fractions. First-pass tuning.
